title: Discovery Service: Enrollment Token Issuance and Approval Workflow for Sentinel Onboarding
description: Implement the admin workflow for generating one-time enrollment tokens that authorize a new sentinel (or re-onboarding sentinel) to register with the Discovery Service. Tokens are short-lived JWTs cryptographically bound to a specific service/role/environment. The approval gate for production environments is enforced by RBAC. Only the hashed token is persisted — the raw token is shown once.
objectives: One-time, short-expiry tokens with atomic consumption preventing any replay or double-use
Approval gate required before prod tokens become active (RBAC security-admin)
Complete audit trail for token creation, approval, consumption, and expiry

steps: Step 1 - Token Creation Endpoint: POST /api/v1/sentinels/enrollments. Requires operator role. Request body: { service_id, role, env, expires_in_seconds (default 600, max 3600), constraints: { allowed_ips?: string[], instance_metadata_hint?: string } }. Generate a 256-bit secure random jti. Build JWT payload: { jti, service_id, role, env, iss: DISCOVERY_DID, iat, exp: iat + expires_in }. Sign with Discovery issuer key from SecretStorage (Ed25519/ES256). Compute SHA-256 hash of the raw JWT (token_hash). Persist entry in enrollment_tokens table with status='PENDING' (prod) or 'APPROVED' (dev/test direct flow). Return token JWT once — never again — in the 201 response.

Step 2 - DB Persistence: enrollment_tokens table (defined in TASK-022). On insert: jti (TEXT UNIQUE), service_id, role, env, status ('PENDING'|'APPROVED'|'CONSUMED'|'EXPIRED'), token_hash (SHA-256 hex), created_by (admin sub), expires_at (from JWT exp), created_at. Fields approved_by and approved_at are NULL until approval. Index on jti. Index on (status, expires_at) for expiry sweep job.

Step 3 - Token Display (One-Time): Return 201 with { token: '<raw_jwt>', token_id: '<uuid>', expires_at, status }. Include header Warning: 'This token will not be shown again. Store it securely.' After this response, the raw token is NEVER returned again — only token_id and status are accessible via GET endpoints. The UI must show a copy-to-clipboard modal with a confirmation checkbox.

Step 4 - Approval Endpoint: POST /api/v1/sentinels/enrollments/{token_id}/approve. Requires security-admin role. Check: status must be PENDING, expires_at must be > now(). Update: status='APPROVED', approved_by=current_user.sub, approved_at=now(). Write audit event: action='approve_enrollment_token', actor=current_user, target_id=token_id, with service_id/role/env in summary. Return 200 with updated token metadata (no raw token).

Step 5 - Token Rejection/Cancellation: POST /api/v1/sentinels/enrollments/{token_id}/cancel. Requires operator role. Status must be PENDING or APPROVED (not CONSUMED/EXPIRED). Sets status='EXPIRED', cancelled_at=now(). Audit logged. This is effectively a pre-use revocation.

Step 6 - Query Endpoints: GET /api/v1/sentinels/enrollments — paginated list (cursor-based, limit max 100). Filter params: status, env, service_id. Response omits raw token. GET /api/v1/sentinels/enrollments/{token_id} — returns metadata only (no raw token).

Step 7 - Token Expiry Sweeper: Background task (runs every 60 seconds via APScheduler or Arq). Query: SELECT id FROM enrollment_tokens WHERE status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED') AND expires_at < now(). Batch update status='EXPIRED'. Log count of expired tokens. No audit event per-token (bulk expiry logged at system level).

Step 8 - Rate Limiting: POST /api/v1/sentinels/enrollments — max 20 requests per admin per minute. Enforced by Redis sliding window counter via key ratelimit:admin:{sub}:enrollment_create. Return 429 with Retry-After header on exceed.

Step 9 - Input Validation: service_id must match ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9\-_]{1,62}[a-z0-9]$ and must exist in services table with matching env. role must be in ['producer', 'consumer']. env must be in ['dev', 'test', 'prod']. expires_in_seconds: min 60, max 3600. Reject any extra unknown fields (strict Pydantic models with model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid')).

Step 10 - Environment-based Auto-Approval: If env in ['dev', 'test'] AND auto_approve_non_prod=true in settings, automatically set status='APPROVED' on creation (skip approval step). This must be disabled in prod — startup check: if env='prod' and auto_approve_non_prod=true → raise startup error.

files.services/discovery/src/discovery/service: Business logic: create, approve, cancel, sweep

fields.name: token_hash
fields.comment: SHA-256 hex of raw JWT — raw token never stored

response_201.note: Token will not be shown again. Copy it now.

error_handling.condition: service_id not found or env mismatch
token already consumed or expired when approving
error_handling.message: Token is in status CONSUMED and cannot be approved
Too many enrollment requests. Retry after 60s
auto_approve_non_prod must not be enabled for prod environment

security_constraints: Raw JWT token stored only in response body of creation call — never persisted in DB nor included in any log line
token_hash (SHA-256 hex) used for verification at onboarding time — compared against hash of submitted token
Admin endpoints (create, approve, cancel) require valid OIDC/JWT token with appropriate role (TASK-023)
All token operations write to audit_events with actor identity, action, target, and summary
Rate limiting on creation: Redis sliding window, 20 req/min per admin sub
expires_in_seconds hard-bounded at 3600 — no indefinite tokens
In prod: status starts as PENDING and requires explicit approval — enforced at service layer, not just UI layer

acceptance_criteria: Token creation returns raw JWT exactly once in 201 response; subsequent GET never returns it
Two concurrent attempts to consume the same token — only one succeeds (tested with concurrent test requests)
In prod env, token with status=PENDING is rejected at onboarding until status=APPROVED
Approval endpoint requires security-admin role; operator cannot approve
All token lifecycle events appear in audit_events table with actor sub and timestamp
Token expiry sweeper sets EXPIRED status correctly after exp time passes
